description: American street gang founded in Chicago in the late 1950s by Eugene Hairston and Jeff Fort. Originally the Blackstone Rangers, it later adopted an Islamic faction called El Rukn. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, murder, and robbery, and is part of the People Nation alliance. It is considered Chicago's most powerful street gang and has been linked to terrorism conspiracies with Libya.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Primarily concentrated in Chicago, some areas of NW Indiana, Michigan, Norfolk, Virginia, Richmond, Virginia and the neighborhood of Rockford Illinois ... and parts of Baldwin Village in Los Angeles
country_links.context: The organization is based and operates in multiple U.S. states including Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Virginia, and California.
